description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the generic docker image and logs into the GitLab Container Registry before each job via `docker login -u $CI_REGISTRY_USER -p $CI_JOB_TOKEN $CI_REGISTRY`. Define a single `test` stage containing five jobs named `test:cypress-ibo-data`, `test:django-server`, `test:docker-compose-a-dev`, `test:pre-commit`, and `test:pytest`, all marked interruptible. In the `test:cypress-ibo-data` job set variables `CYPRESS_SPEC_SCRIPT`, `DJANGO_SERVER_SCRIPT`, `ENV_FILENAME`, and `REGISTRY`, run `COMPOSE_BAKE=true docker compose -f docker/docker-compose-cypress.yml up --build --abort-on-container-exit` then `docker compose -f docker/docker-compose-cypress.yml down --volumes`, and in after_script create `$CI_PROJECT_DIR/screenshots` and `$CI_PROJECT_DIR/pdfs`, copy the generated files, and publish them as artifacts with paths to those directories and JUnit reports from `$CI_PROJECT_DIR/e2e/cypress/reports/*.xml` always. The `test:django-server`, `test:pre-commit`, and `test:pytest` jobs each depend on the `build:docker-images` job, set the `REGISTRY` variable to `registry.gitlab.com/oly-exams/exam_tools`, and execute `docker run ${REGISTRY}/<image-name>:${CI_COMMIT_SHA}-untested` where `<image-name>` is `django-server-test`, `pre-commit`, or `pytest` respectively. The `test:docker-compose-a-dev` job also sets `REGISTRY` to the same value, runs `COMPOSE_BAKE=true docker compose -f docker/docker-compose-a-dev.yml up --build --wait` followed by `docker compose -f docker/docker-compose-a-dev.yml down --volumes`, and has no explicit dependencies.
